commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(sandbox): mount workspace skills read-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #17931.

This PR protects skill paths exposed to Docker sandboxes from in-container mutation.

For `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, project skill roots are mounted read-only over the writable workspace:

- `<workspace>/skills` -> `/workspace/skills:ro,z`
- `<workspace>/.agents/skills` -> `/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z`

For `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `"none"`, this PR does not add a separate `/workspace/skills` bind. `/workspace` is already mounted read-only in those modes, so synced skills inherit the read-only workspace mount. This avoids stale nested bind mounts when skill sync removes and recreates the staged `skills/` directory.

## What changed

- Added read-only Docker overlays for project skill roots in `rw` mode.
- Kept `ro` and `none` protection on the existing read-only `/workspace` mount instead of adding a nested `/workspace/skills` bind.
- Removed the chmod-based copied-skill protection path because chmod is not a sandbox boundary and can interfere with refresh.
- Added stable read-only skill mount hash state so `rw` containers created before skill directories exist are not considered current after those directories appear.
- Bumped `SANDBOX_MOUNT_FORMAT_VERSION` from `2` to `3`.
- Added deterministic mount tests for `rw`, `ro`, and `none` behavior.

## Security impact

This prevents sandboxed Docker agents from rewriting the skill instructions they are executing under.

For `rw` users, normal workspace writes still work, but writes under protected skill paths now fail. This is an intentional compatibility-affecting hardening for workspace skill directories.

For `ro` and `none`, the synced skill staging tree remains refreshable from the host side, while the container sees those files through the existing read-only `/workspace` mount.

## Validation

Targeted Vitest command:

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts \
  src/agents/skills.build-workspace-skills-prompt.syncs-merged-skills-into-target-workspace.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** This PR protects workspace skill instructions exposed to Docker sandboxes from changes made inside the sandbox while keeping the normal writable workspace writable. In rw mode, /workspace remains writable while /workspace/skills and /workspace/.agents/skills are overlaid as read-only bind mounts. In ro and none modes, the existing read-only /workspace mount provides the protection without a stale nested /workspace/skills bind.

**Real environment tested:** Repository openclaw/openclaw, PR #85591, branch jason-allen-oneal:fix/sandbox-readonly-skill-mounts, current PR head a617ec32e981348c4689fa5590662703fe9ebbe9. Docker proof was captured on the code change before the empty check-retrigger commit, using Docker image openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim with host paths redacted.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Created an rw Docker sandbox with /workspace writable and both skill roots mounted read-only. Inspected the effective mounts. Attempted writes under /workspace/skills and /workspace/.agents/skills. Created an ro Docker sandbox with /workspace mounted read-only and no nested /workspace/skills bind. Replaced the staged skills directory on the host while the ro sandbox was still running. Verified the running container saw the refreshed skill content and still blocked writes under /workspace/skills.

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture from a real Docker sandbox run showed /workspace as RW=true, /workspace/skills as RW=false, and /workspace/.agents/skills as RW=false. The same capture showed write attempts under /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md and /workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md failing with Read-only file system. In the ro sandbox proof, terminal output showed staged skill content changing from version=one to version=two after a host-side refresh while the running sandbox remained read-only for /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md.

**Observed result after fix:** In rw mode, /workspace stayed writable, both skill paths were read-only, and writes under both skill paths failed with Read-only file system. In ro mode, there was no nested /workspace/skills bind, the read-only /workspace mount blocked writes, and a host-side staged skills refresh became visible inside the already-running sandbox.

**What was not tested:** A full interactive UI session was not included. This proof covers the Docker mount behavior directly, and the targeted Vitest command covers deterministic mount and sandbox configuration behavior.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.author: altaywtf

target.title: fix(ui): preserve WebChat messages across session switches
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #80855.
- Cache visible WebChat messages per session when switching conversations, then restore cached messages immediately when switching back before chat.history returns.
- Cache final assistant messages for switched-away sessions so subagent/report events are not dropped while another conversation is selected.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: WebChat session switching should not leave the restored conversation blank, and final assistant/subagent messages for a switched-away session should not be silently dropped.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with production TypeScript UI modules executed through Node 22.22.2 and tsx; no test runner or mocks were used for the proof commands.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran two node --import tsx commands from the repository root. One imported switchChatSession from ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts and switched main -> other -> main with real session state. The other imported handleChatEvent from ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts and delivered a final event for agent:main:webchat while main was selected.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e '<imports switchChatSession from ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts and switches main -> other -> main>'
{
  "activeSession": "main",
  "visibleMessagesAfterSwitchBack": [
    "main report before switch"
  ],
  "cachedOtherMessages": [
    "other reply before switch back"
  ]
}
```

```text
$ node --import tsx -e '<imports handleChatEvent from ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts and sends final event for agent:main:webchat while main is selected>'
{
  "result": null,
  "currentSession": "main",
  "currentMessages": [
    "main visible"
  ],
  "cachedWebchatMessages": [
    "subagent report while switched away"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Switching back to main immediately restored "main report before switch" instead of an empty message list, and a final message for the switched-away WebChat session was retained in cachedWebchatMessages while the currently visible main session stayed unchanged.
- What was not tested: No browser screenshot or live multi-channel WSL2 deployment was captured; the proof exercises the production UI state-transition modules directly.

## Verification
- RED: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts failed on the new switch-back preservation test before implementation.
- RED: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts failed on the new switched-away final-event cache test before implementation.
- GREEN: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
- GREEN: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
- GREEN: PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-shims:$PATH pnpm check:changed -- --base origin/main
- GREEN: git diff --check
target.author: luoyanglang
